---
title: "Power Analysis"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-11"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Clear environment
```{r, include=FALSE}

rm(list = ls(all.names = TRUE))
```

Install packages
```{r}
install.packages(c(
  "pwr",
  "lme4",
  "simr",
  "effectsize"
))
```

Load libraries
```{r, include=FALSE}
library(pwr)
library(lme4)
library(simr)
library(effectsize)
```

Power Analysis for 80% Power
```{r}

# Parameters based on pilot data
alpha <- 0.05
target_power <- 0.80

# Get effect size from pilot data
# Calculate Cohen's d for Hit/Miss between conditions
# Example: if you have two conditions to compare

# For t-test (two independent groups)
# pwr.t.test(n = , d = , sig.level = , power = , type = , alternative = )

# Example: if effect size d = 0.5 (medium effect)
# pwr.t.test(d = 0.5, power = 0.80, type = "two.sample", alternative = "two.sided")

# For ANOVA (multiple groups)
# pwr.anova.test(k = , n = , f = , sig.level = , power = )

# Example: if effect size f = 0.25 (medium effect), 3 groups
# pwr.anova.test(k = 3, f = 0.25, power = 0.80)

# For linear mixed models using simr
# powerSim(model, nsim = 100)

```

Calculate effect size from pilot data
```{r}

# Load pilot data to calculate effect sizes
path  <- "~/labrotation.si@gmail.com - Google Drive/My Drive/Hasib Thesis/ExperimentDataConvert/dataAnalysis/dataFiles"

files <-list.files(path, pattern = "\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE, recursive = FALSE)
names(files) <- basename(files)

read_one <- function(f){
  df <- read_csv(f, show_col_types = FALSE,
                 na = c("", "NA", "NaN"),
                 locale = locale(encoding = "UTF-8"),
                 guess_max = 100000)
  if ("ID" %in% names(df)) mutate(df, ID = as.character(.data$ID)) else df
}

DT <- map_dfr(files, read_one, .id = "source_file") %>%
  relocate(source_file, .before = 1)

# Calculate effect size (Cohen's d) for Hit rate between conditions
hit_by_condition <- DT %>%
  filter(signal_detection == "Hit") %>%
  group_by(ID, condition) %>%
  summarise(n_trials = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = condition, values_from = n_trials, id_cols = ID, values_fill = 0)

# Calculate means and SD for each condition
condition_means <- DT %>%
  filter(signal_detection == "Hit") %>%
  group_by(condition) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_hit = n(),
    sd_hit = NA,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Cohen's d calculation
# d = (mean1 - mean2) / pooled_sd

# Calculate required sample size for 80% power
# Using pwr.t.test for two-sample t-test
# pwr.t.test(d = effect_size, power = 0.80, type = "two.sample")

```

Calculate required sample size
```{r}

# For two-sample t-test
# Replace d with your calculated effect size
effect_size_d <- 0.5  # Example: medium effect

pwr_result <- pwr.t.test(
  d = effect_size_d,
  power = 0.80,
  sig.level = 0.05,
  type = "two.sample",
  alternative = "two.sided"
)

pwr_result

# Required sample size per group
n_per_group <- ceiling(pwr_result$n)
total_n <- n_per_group * 2

cat("Required sample size per group:", n_per_group, "\n")
cat("Total required sample size:", total_n, "\n")

# For ANOVA (if more than 2 conditions)
# k = number of groups
# f = effect size (Cohen's f)

# pwr.anova.test(k = 3, f = 0.25, power = 0.80)

```

Power analysis for mixed effects model
```{r}

# If using linear mixed effects model
# Calculate effect size for lmer

# Example model
# lmer_model <- lmer(signal_detection ~ condition + (1|ID), data = DT)

# Using simr for power analysis of mixed models
# powerSim(lmer_model, nsim = 100)

# For extension: vary sample size
# powerCurve(lmer_model, nsim = 100, along = "ID")

```

Summary
```{r}

# Print summary of power analysis
cat("=== Power Analysis Summary ===\n")
cat("Target power: 80%\n")
cat("Significance level (alpha): 0.05\n")
cat("Effect size (d):", effect_size_d, "\n")
cat("Required n per group:", n_per_group, "\n")
cat("Total required n:", total_n, "\n")

```
